summary: M7 (escalation) scrutiny pass clean. Four features shipped: escalation-rules-schema, escalation-runtime-trigger-eval, escalation-ui-dashboard, escalation-widget-actions. Automated validators green.

automatedValidators.testSuite: pnpm vitest run — 555 passed / 1 skipped / 67 files (+64 new across the milestone).

review.correctness: pass — table + RLS + cascade + conversations.needs_human. CHECK constraints cover three rule_types + five actions + jsonb_typeof object-only for config/action_config.
review.security: pass — four owner-scoped RLS policies; widget never reads directly; runtime evaluator uses service-role.
review.quality: pass — compound index (site_id, is_active, priority DESC, created_at ASC) mirrors M6F1 pattern; partial index on needs_human keeps scans tight.
review.testing: pass — 14 integration tests including all 5 actions, CHECK violations, peer isolation, cascade, needs_human persistence.

review.correctness: pass — evaluator iterates by priority DESC, created_at ASC; turn_count / keyword / intent all covered; classifier fires at most once per evaluation; preClassifiedIntent feed-forward prevents redundant gpt-4o-mini calls when the response matcher already classified.
review.security: pass — pure evaluator; no new secrets; session route already auths.
review.quality: pass — self-contained module with exported types; \x1E trailer protocol is a minimal addition with zero breaking changes to existing text-stream behavior.
review.consistency: pass — reuses M6F2 triggerMatches so keyword semantics are identical to response rules.
review.testing: pass — 14 unit tests (including turn_count non-numeric config, classifier-once-for-multiple-intent-rules) + 5 stream-integration tests proving canned + RAG trailers and handoff needs_human flag.

review.correctness: pass — validateRuleBody enforces per-rule-type config shape and per-action action_config shape; PATCH merges with the existing row before re-validating; reorder endpoint assigns descending priorities matching the runtime order-by.
review.security: pass — RLS gates all CRUD; reorder's bulk update silently no-ops on invisible ids.
review.quality: pass — DraftRule is a wide typed view so switching trigger/action types preserves form state across toggles; toast feedback on every mutation.
review.consistency: pass — follows M6F3 UI layout + patterns.
review.testing: pass — 18 API unit tests covering every validation branch + three happy-path creates mapping to VAL-ESCAL-002/003/004.

review.correctness: pass — stream parsing strips \x1E trailer live; pending_action dispatches to the right renderer; show_form promotes email/phone/name while preserving extra_fields. leads route handles phone-only + escalation source correctly.
review.security: pass — calendly iframe guards against non-http URLs; widget honeypot preserved; leads route validates email format + source enum.
review.quality: pass — pure helpers (parseStream, buildShowFormPayload) extracted to widget/src/escalation-protocol.ts for testability; CSS respects prefers-reduced-motion.
review.consistency: pass — CSS tokens match the existing widget style; new .rc-escalation-* classes follow the naming convention.
review.testing: pass — 8 protocol helper tests + 5 extended leads route tests covering source + phone-only insert + extra_fields preservation.

libraryUpdates: architecture.md unchanged — escalation slots into the existing chat request path between RAG and conversation-save. environment.md unchanged — no new env vars.
leads table schema change documented in the M7F4 handoff; dashboard conversations view now surfaces needs_human.
conventionsAdded: Stream trailer protocol: \x1E (ASCII Record Separator) delimits the optional JSON trailer carrying {pending_action}. Consumers split on the last \x1E and parse the rest as JSON; malformed JSON is a non-fatal warning.
leads.email NULLable with a partial unique index WHERE email IS NOT NULL — convention for optional-identifier columns going forward.
